global.title: Analytics dimension paths use slash-delimited hierarchies with LOD parameter controlling aggregation specificity across dimensions
global.content: In the Analytics Engine, `AnalyticsDimension` objects have a `path` field using `/`-delimited hierarchical strings (like URIs). Querying with path `/atlas/mip40` matches all dimensions starting with that prefix. The LOD (level of detail) parameter controls result aggregation: LOD 0 groups all results into a single value; LOD 1 groups by the first path segment; LOD 2 by the first two segments, etc. LOD greater than the number of path parts appends `/*` wildcards but returns the same results as the highest meaningful level. Best practice: start with general queries (high LOD, broad path) and narrow down to specific paths and lower LODs for particular metrics. There will be thousands/millions of series objects but far fewer dimension objects.
global.description: Paths like /atlas/mip40/MIP40c3SP61 determine query scope; LOD 0 groups all results, increasing LOD increases aggregation specificity.
